_comment: Cross-scanner credential parity corpus. Tests that both egress and ingress scanners detect the same credential patterns. Documented divergences are intentional.

cases.note: Both detect. Ingress lacks \b boundary — intentionally broader for redaction.
Egress contextual tier (placeholder bypass); ingress always redacts.

Egress blocks. Ingress triggers SuppressedText (full suppression) — not counted as a redaction. ingress_redacts=false because redact_known_secrets count stays 0; PEM is caught by contains_pem_private_key() before redact_known_secrets runs.
Both detect bearer auth. Token is 23 chars (>= 20 minimum).

cases.input: This is a basic setup guide for authentication.
cases.note: After auth-header split, ingress should NOT redact 'basic setup'. If ingress_redacts is true, the auth regex is too broad.
Egress shadows (broad tier, no block); ingress redacts.
Intentional divergence: ingress detects URL userinfo credentials; egress has no URL userinfo family (allow). Ingress is more conservative here.

cases.input: Fix the flaky test in auth_test.py
cases.note: No credentials — both scanners should pass clean.
